heading: Reports to Congress
label: 4107 Reports to Congress
text: Not later than six months after its organization, and every six months thereafter, the Commission shall make a report to the Congress concerning its operations under this subchapter.1See Modification of Reporting Requirements note below.1
notes: Annual Submission of Reports, , , modified the provisions of this section, beginning , to require annual instead of semiannual submission to Congress by Foreign Claims Settlement Commission of report concerning its operations under War Claims Act of 1948.Pub. L. 89–348, § 2(6)79 Stat. 1312Nov. 8, 1965Jan. 1, 1967

References in TextThis subchapter, referred to in text, was in the original “this title”, meaning title I of , , which is classified principally to this subchapter. For complete classification of title I to the Code, see Tables.act July 3, 1948, ch. 82662 Stat. 1240
CodificationSection was formerly classified to section 2008 of the former Appendix to this title prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.
Amendments1962— made technical amendment to reference in original act which appears in text as reference to this subchapter.Pub. L. 87–846

Transfer of FunctionsFor provisions transferring Foreign Claims Settlement Commission of the United States to Department of Justice, as a separate agency, see section 1622a et seq. of Title 22, Foreign Relations and Intercourse.

Modification of Reporting Requirements, , , modified the reporting requirements of this section as follows:Pub. L. 89–348, § 2(6)79 Stat. 1312Nov. 8, 1965“Beginning , from semiannual to annual submission to the Congress by the Foreign Claims Settlement Commission of the United States of a report concerning its operations under the War Claims Act of 1948 (; 50 U.S.C. App. 2008 [now ]).”January 1, 196762 Stat. 124650 U.S.C. 4107

Transfer of FunctionsWar Claims Commission, including offices of its members, abolished and functions transferred to Foreign Claims Settlement Commission of the United States by Reorg. Plan No. 1 of 1954, §§ 2, 4, eff. , 19 F.R. 3985, , set out in the Appendix to Title 5, Government Organization and Employees. See, also, and notes thereunder.July 1, 195468 Stat. 1279section 4101 of this title
